title: Cabinet, Ministers, and the Public Service
shortSummary: See how political authority and administrative capacity work together. This is where campaigns stop mattering and government starts mattering.
whyItMatters: People often think politics happens in Parliament alone. In practice, a large amount of real governing happens through Cabinet processes, ministerial offices, and the APS.
mentalModels: Cabinet chooses direction; the APS builds feasible options
Ministers are accountable for portfolios they do not personally operate
A government can win politics and still fail implementation

goDeeper.label: Move into policy process
goDeeper.description: Follow the chain from executive intent into real policy machinery.

goDeeper.label: See the APS policy officer day-in-the-life
goDeeper.description: Ground the institutional model in an actual working week.
